Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

ŗģģŗĮÜŗģĮŗĮćŗģĮŗģŅŗģĮŗģ≤ŗĮć)|2=it|3=Legno (elemento)|4=es|5=Madera (Wu Xing)|6=tr|7=Tahta (klasik element)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (śú®)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

ŗģģŗĮÜŗģĮŗĮćŗģĮŗģŅŗģĮŗģ≤ŗĮć)|2=it|3=Legno (elemento)|4=es|5=Madera (Wu Xing)|6=tr|7=Tahta (klasik element)|1=Wood (wuxing)|lt=Wood (śú®)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

Stems]].<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=4c9a92862e1b9aabc3b009cfd0a01023a23ad178c0d8269211d19906d318e4d6JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kinoto+(%e4%b9%99)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qaXRlbm9uLmNvbS93b3JkLzUwNTI&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=1be7966818ee6798e5c1fdd09171ddb9d95fab26ae826d08015e33742959ef07JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kinoto+(%e4%b9%99)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD0xNzI0MA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=4f4babef6238b7c7cd9b5847a6f52b83af1f7888cd92e4da51952307ac659dd0JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kinoto+(%e4%b9%99)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkvJUU0JUI5JTk5LSVFMyU4MSU4RCVFMyU4MSVBRSVFMyU4MSVBOA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=8a1a36e31997673c3990a8a25fe11ee979cd2b1283c0c44659ae224f81bb0656JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kinoto+(%e4%b9%99)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cud29yZHNlbnNlLmV1L2tpbm90by8&ntb=1</ref>

Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).
== Earthly Branches Containing Yi (šĻô) == {{Wiktionary|šĻô}} {| class="wikitable alternance droite" |{{ill|Yin Wood Ox|ja|šĻôšłĎ|1=Yin Wood Ox|zh|šĻôšłĎ|1=Yin Wood Ox|ko|žĚĄž∂ē|fr|Buffle de bois|de|Holz-B√ľffel|en|Wood Ox|lt=šĻôšłĎ (Yin Wood Ox)|lt_ko=žĚĄž∂ē|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Wood Ox|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Wood Ox|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin Wood Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of [[:en:ShŇćchŇę|shochu]], there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the [[:en:Common Era|Common Era]] correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

According to the {{ill|Yin-Yang Five Elements theory|ja|ťôįťôĹšļĒŤ°ĆŤ™¨|1=Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang Five Elements theory|13=User:Immanuelle/Yin-Yang and Wuxing and Wuxing Theory|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ťôįťôĹšļĒŤ°ĆśÄĚśÉ≥}}, it is assigned to the Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of {{ill|ShŇćchŇę|en|ShŇćchŇę|WD=Q1165352|lt=shochu}}, there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era}} correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

Hence, in Japan, it is also referred to as "kinoto" (meaning "younger brother of wood").
== Pronunciations: šĻô (Stem 2) == * Chinese (Pinyin): Y«ź * Korean: Eul (žĚĄ) * Vietnamese: Šļ§t * Japanese: ** On‚Äôyomi: Otsu („Āä„Ā§) ** Kunyomi (elemental): Kinoto („Āć„Āģ„Ā®, śú®„ĀģŚľü ‚Äď ‚ÄúYounger Wood‚ÄĚ)
== Overview == * The origin of the kanji "šĻô" has been subject to various proposals, but no definitive theory has been established.<ref>{{Citation| author=Yu Shengwu| year=1996| title=Jiaguwen Zhilin (ÁĒ≤ť™®śĖáŚ≠óŤ©Āśěó)| location=Beijing| publisher=Zhonghua Book Company| isbn=7-101-01430-5| pages=1178‚Äď80|qq=}}</ref> The character is used as a borrowed form to denote the second in the sequence of the Ten Heavenly Stems. * When the Ten Heavenly Stems are used for Ranking, "Otsu" signifies the second position. It is also used in classification systems, such as "Otsu-type" (šĻôÁ®ģ) or "Otsu-class" (šĻôť°ě). While these terms are simply labels for categories, many mistakenly believe that "Otsu" implies inferiority compared to "Kou" (ÁĒ≤, the first stem). For example, in the classification of {{ill|ShŇćchŇę|en|ShŇćchŇę|WD=Q1165352|lt=shochu}}, there are "Kou-class" and "Otsu-class" varieties. To avoid the misconception that "Otsu-class" is inferior, it is often referred to as "honkaku shochu" (śú¨ś†ľÁĄľťÖé), meaning "authentic shochu." * Additionally, expressions like "difficult to determine between Kou and Otsu" (ÁĒ≤šĻôšĽė„ĀĎ„ĀĆ„Āü„ĀĄ) suggest a comparison where "Kou" is perceived as superior and "Otsu" as lesser. In {{ill|calligraphy|ja|śõłťĀď|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/calligraphy|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=East Asian calligraphy|WD=Q11054563}} and other disciplines, these terms are used to indicate progress or Ranking in proficiency tests. * Years ending with the digit 5 in the {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=Common Era}} correspond to the year of "Otsu." The alternate name for such years is "{{ill|Zhanmeng|ja|śóÉŤíô|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zhanmeng|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}."<ref>{{Cite web |title=Ancient Alternative Names and Explanations for the Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches |url=https://www.cidianwang.com/shufazuopin/jiaocheng/932596.htm |website=www.cidianwang.com |access-date=2024-03-08|qq=}}</ref> * The opposite counterpart of "Otsu" is {{ill|Kanoe (Śļö)|cdo|Gńēng (tińēng-gńÉng)|fr|Genggan|gan|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|hak|K√Ęng (thi√™n-k√īn)|ja|Kanoe|ko|Í≤Ĺ (ž≤úÍįĄ)|vi|Canh (Thi√™n can)|za|Geng (diengan)|zh-min-nan|Ke‚ĀŅ (Thian-kan)|zh-yue|Śļö (Ś§©ŚĻ≤)|zh|Śļö|1=Kanoe (Śļö)|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|2=ja|3=Śļö|4=zh|5=Śļö|lt=Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanoe (Śļö)|qq=}}. * The {{ill|lucky direction|ja|śĀĶśĖĻ|3=ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě|1=Toshitoku-shin|lt=Lucky direction|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/lucky direction|13=User:Immanuelle/Toshitoku-shin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ś≠≥Śĺ≥Á•ě}} (eho) associated with Otsu is slightly west of southwest (255 degrees).

{{Ten Heavenly Stems and Earthly Branches}} '''Kinoto''' (šĻô) is the second of the {{ill|Heavenly Stems|en|Heavenly Stems|WD=Q1190985|lt=Ten Heavenly Stems}}.<ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=4c9a92862e1b9aabc3b009cfd0a01023a23ad178c0d8269211d19906d318e4d6JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kinoto+(%e4%b9%99)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qaXRlbm9uLmNvbS93b3JkLzUwNTI&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=1be7966818ee6798e5c1fdd09171ddb9d95fab26ae826d08015e33742959ef07JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kinoto+(%e4%b9%99)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGFub3NoaWlqYXBhbmVzZS5jb20vZGljdGlvbmFyeS9lbnRyeV9kZXRhaWxzLmNmbT9lbnRyeV9pZD0xNzI0MA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=4f4babef6238b7c7cd9b5847a6f52b83af1f7888cd92e4da51952307ac659dd0JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kinoto+(%e4%b9%99)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly9qbGVhcm4ubmV0L2RpY3Rpb25hcnkvJUU0JUI5JTk5LSVFMyU4MSU4RCVFMyU4MSVBRSVFMyU4MSVBOA&ntb=1</ref><ref>https://www.bing.com/ck/a?!&&p=8a1a36e31997673c3990a8a25fe11ee979cd2b1283c0c44659ae224f81bb0656JmltdHM9MTc0MzAzMzYwMA&ptn=3&ver=2&hsh=4&fclid=3e6f989f-7779-62d7-1cd7-8d247648633d&psq=Kinoto+(%e4%b9%99)&u=a1aHR0cHM6Ly93d3cud29yZHNlbnNlLmV1L2tpbm90by8&ntb=1</ref>
